headline: Cape Verde Fishers and Ocean Guardians Work Hand in Hand
meta_description: A decade-long program pairs fishing communities with marine protection, creating sustainable livelihoods and healthier seas.
summary_short: For 10 years, fishers in Fogo, Cabo Verde have balanced their work with ocean monitoring, protecting marine ecosystems.
body:
On the island of Fogo in the Cabo Verde archipelago, fishing families like Alberto Martius and his son Leandro have become guardians of the sea they depend on. For a decade, a local program has woven marine protection into the fabric of daily fishing life, asking fishers to watch over ocean health while pursuing their catch.

This partnership recognizes a simple truth: those who work the waters daily are best positioned to notice what matters. By valuing fishers as both stewards and livelihoods providers, the initiative shows how economic survival and environmental care can strengthen each other rather than compete. Ten years of commitment demonstrates that sustainable practices take root when communities lead the way.
